title: Besan Toast (Gram Flour Toast)

subs.name: potato slices (parboiled) or thick raw banana slices for a no-bread version
rice flour plus 1 tbsp cornflour for a crispier, gluten-free coating
subs.notes: the texture will be lighter and crunchier

ingredients.name: water
visual_checks: adjust to make a thin coating batter
ingredients.notes: adjust to make a thin coating batter

subs.name: a pinch of black pepper for less heat

steps.text: In a wide bowl, whisk the besan, water, turmeric, red chilli powder, salt and cumin seeds into a smooth, lump-free batter — it should have the consistency of thin pancake batter, thick enough to coat a bread slice without dripping off immediately. Stir in the chopped onion, green chilli and coriander leaves.

Heat 1.5 tbsp oil in a wide tawa or frying pan on medium flame. While the oil heats, dip two bread slices into the batter one at a time, letting them soak for 3–4 seconds per side so the batter penetrates slightly.

Lay the coated slices in the hot oil. Cook for 2–2.5 minutes per side until deep golden and crisp — the besan forms a savoury, slightly crispy crust. Flip gently with a flat spatula. Fry the remaining slices in the second batch, adding the remaining oil before they go in.
Drain on a plate lined with kitchen paper or newspaper. Serve immediately with tomato ketchup, green chutney or a small bowl of curd. The toast loses its crunch quickly, so eat at once.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: besan → rice flour plus 1 tbsp cornflour for a crispier, gluten-free coating (the texture will be lighter and crunchier); bread → potato slices (parboiled) or thick raw banana slices for a no-bread version; green-chilli → a pinch of black pepper for less heat
